# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  DOCKER_DRIVER: overlay2d
  
merge_requests:
  stage: 'test' # trigger on merge requests, not default events (i.e., just push)
  before_script:
    - apt-get update --quiet || true; apt-get install docker git curl python3 jq gh
      ansible sshpass libcap2 unzip pwgen rsync socat netcat iputils-ping strace lsof bind-utils openssl sed awk bc util-linux less nfs-common -y --quiet  # Installing necessary tools for CI/CD pipeline.  
      echo $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY | docker login ghcr.io -u gitlab-ci-token --password=$CI_JOB_TOKEN --email=gitlab@example.com     # Log in to Docker Hub registry (github).  For CI/CD pipeline, it should be `ghcr.io` .
    - echo $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY | docker login ghcr.io  >> /dev/null         # Login into GitHub Container Registry for release notes generation and deployment to Github Packages (Release Notes).  For CI/CD pipeline, it should be `ghcr.io` .
    - mkdir ~/.ssh && echo $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY > ~/.ssh/idrsa; chmod 600 ~/.ssh/idrsa   # Generate SSH key pair for GitLab CI and Docker Hub registry login to avoid need of password.     .
    - eval "$(ssh-agent)" && echo $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY | ssh-add --apple-use-keychain >> /dev/null  # Add the private SSH key into agent for GitLab CI and Docker Hub registry login to avoid need of password.  
    - gh auth login                                   # Log in as an authorized user with `gh` CLI tool, so we can publish packages using token authentication (optional).     .      
      echo $CI_JOB_TOKEN | docker login --username gitlab-ci-token –password=~/idrsa   >> /dev/null   # Log in to Docker Hub registry for CI. For release notes, it should be `gitlab-ci-token` .
      echo $CI_JOB_TOKEN | docker login ghcr.io --username gitlab-ci-token –password=~/.ssh/idrsa  >> /dev/null # Log in to GitHub Container Registry for release notes generation and deployment (optional). For CI, it should be `gitlab-ci-token` .
    - echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' -f1 –unique >> MRTitle.txt   # Extract the first word of commit message as title for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).     ..grep "^[a-zA-Z0-9]"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f2- >> MRDescription.txt    # Extract the rest of words from commit message as description for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "[a-zA-Z0-9].*"
     echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f1 >> MRTitle.txt    # Extract the first word of commit message as title for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "^[a-zA-Z0-9]"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f2 >> MRDescription.txt    # Extract the rest of words from commit message as description for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "[a-zA-Z0-9].*"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f1 >> MRTitle.txt    # Extract the first word of commit message as title for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "^[a-zA-Z0-9]"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f2 >> MRDescription.txt    # Extract the rest of words from commit message as description for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "[a-zA-Z0-9].*"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f1 >> MRTitle.txt    # Extract the first word of commit message as title for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "^[a-zA-Z0-9]"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f2 >> MRDescription.txt    # Extract the rest of words from commit message as description for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "[a-zA-Z0-9].*"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f1 >> MRTitle.txt    # Extract the first word of commit message as title for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "^[a-zA-Z0-9]"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f2 >> MRDescription.txt    # Extract the rest of words from commit message as description for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "[a-zA-Z0-9].*"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f1 >> MRTitle.txt    # Extract the first word of commit message as title for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "^[a-zA-Z0-9]"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f2 >> MRDescription.txt    # Extract the rest of words from commit message as description for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "[a-zA-Z0-9].*"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f1 >> MRTitle.txt    # Extract the first word of commit message as title for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "^[a-zA-Z0-9]"
      echo $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE | cut -d' ' –f2 >> MRDescription.txt    # Extract the rest of words from commit message as description for release notes and deployment tags in Github Release Notes section (optional).   ..grep "[a-zA-Z0-9].*"
